bodyFields.claim: Physicalism cannot provide a physical account of phenomenological features because every subjective phenomenon is essentially connected with a single point of view, which objective physical theory abandons.
bodyFields.support: The bat thought experiment illustrates that we cannot form a conception of what it is like to be a bat from an objective standpoint, revealing the failure of physicalism to capture the subjective character of experience.

concepts.title: Bat Thought Experiment

bodyFields.finding: It is impossible to imagine what it is like to be a bat by extrapolating from our own experience; we can only imagine what it would be like for us to behave as a bat.
bodyFields.method: Philosophical reflection on the limitations of imagination using the example of bat echolocation and the attempt to imagine subjective experience from a different sensory apparatus.

relatedConceptNames: What It Is Like To Be

concepts.title: What It Is Like To Be

bodyFields.definition: The subjective character of an organism's experience that is essentially connected to a single point of view.
bodyFields.significance: It is central to the mind-body problem, as it resists objective physical explanation and illustrates the limitations of reductionism in understanding consciousness.
